DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ULISES VALLADARES' MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. NO STATUTORY GROUND FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS EXISTS, BECAUSE NO DISCOVERY MOTION TO COMPEL IS BEFORE THE COURT IN THIS MOTION. CCP 128.7 DOES NOT APPLY TO DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS (CCP SEC. 128.7(G)); SO WITHDRAWAL OF A MOTION TO COMPEL DOES NOT FALL WITHIN THE PROHIBITED CONDUCT COVERED BY SECTION 128.7. FINALLY, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT WITHDRAWAL OF AN IMPROPERLY NOTICED MOTION CONSTITUTES SANCTIONABLE CONDUCT. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE SUBSTANTIVE MOTION TO COMPEL IS PENDING BEFORE THE COURT AT A LATER DATE, AND THE PARTIES MAY ADDRESS DISCOVERY RELATED CONDUCT AND THE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR THE OBJECTIONS AT THAT TIME. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO-TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Answers To Deposition Questions And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Nicole E. Forde, Esq., appearing for Deenant and moving party Bi-Rite Creamery, Inc.
(415) 394 9400

DEFENDANT BI-RITE CREAMERY, INC.'s Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Answers To Deposition Questions And Request For Sanctions is scheduled this day. There being no appearance by the Plaintiff nor an opposition filed, the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling.

Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Answers at Deposition is GRANTED. The parties have met and confereed to continue Plaintiff's deposition on April 6, 2016. The parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open Court.

Judge Pro-tem: Rebecca L. Woodson; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Not Reported.(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF ULISES VALLADARES' Motion For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions is denied. No statutory ground for discovery sanctions exists, because no discovery motion to compel is before the court in this motion. CCP 128.7 does not apply to discovery proceedings (CCP Sec. 128.7(g)); so withdrawal of a motion to compel does not fall within the prohibited conduct covered by Section 128.7. Finally, the court does not find that withdrawal of an improperly noticed motion constitutes sanctionable conduct. The Court notes that the substantive motion to compel is pending before the court at a later date, and the parties may address discovery related conduct and the substantial justification for the objections at that time. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge Pro-tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt
